t.app.title: ⚡ Pixelle-Video - AI Fully Automated Short Video Engine
t.app.subtitle: Powered by Pixelle.AI
t.input.topic_placeholder:
AI auto-creates a specified number of narrations
E.g.: How to increase passive income
t.input.topic_help: Enter a topic and AI will generate content based on it
t.input.text_help_generate: Enter a topic or subject (AI will create narration)
t.input.text_help_fixed: Enter the complete narration script (used as-is, no rewriting)
t.input.text_help_digital: Enter product introduction; if provided, product name is not required
t.input.text_help_audio: Enter a prompt describing the desired scene in detail
t.split.mode_help: Choose how to split the text into video segments
t.input.content_placeholder:
Used as-is, no rewriting (split according to the mode below)
Example:
Hello everyone, today I'd like to share three learning tips.

The first tip is focus training — meditate 10 minutes every day.

The second tip is active recall — repeat what you just learned immediately.
t.input.content_help: Provide your own content for video generation
t.input.title: Title (Optional)
t.voice.title: 🎤 Voice Selection
t.style.workflow_what: Determines how each frame's illustration is generated and its visual effect (e.g., using FLUX, SD, etc.)
t.style.workflow_how: Place exported image_xxx.json workflow files (API format) into workflows/selfhost/ (local ComfyUI) or workflows/runninghub/ (cloud) folders
t.style.video_workflow_what: Determines how each frame's video segment is generated and its visual effect (e.g., different video generation models)
t.style.video_workflow_how: Place exported video_xxx.json workflow files (API format) into workflows/selfhost/ (local ComfyUI) or workflows/runninghub/ (cloud) folders
t.style.prompt_prefix_what: Automatically prepended to all image prompts to control illustration style (e.g., cartoon, realistic, etc.)
t.style.prompt_prefix_how: Fill in the style description directly below. To save permanently, edit config.yaml
t.style.prompt_prefix_placeholder: Enter style prefix (leave empty to use config default)
t.style.prompt_prefix_help: This text will be automatically prepended to all image generation prompts. To modify permanently, edit config.yaml
t.style.description_placeholder: Describe the illustration style you want (any language)...
t.style.test_prompt_help: Enter a test prompt to preview the style
t.template.separator_selected: Please select a specific template, not a group heading
t.template.what: Controls the visual layout and design style of each video frame (title, text, image layout)
t.template.how:
Place .html template files into templates/<size>/ directory (e.g., templates/1080x1920/), the system groups by size automatically. Custom CSS is supported.

**Template Naming Convention**

- `static_*.html` → Static style templates (no AI-generated media)
- `image_*.html` → Illustration templates (AI generates images)
- `video_*.html` → Video templates (AI generates videos)

**Note**

Your computer must have at least one of the following browsers installed:
1. Google Chrome (Windows, macOS)
2. Chromium Browser (Linux)
3. Microsoft Edge
t.template.type.static_hint: Uses the template's built-in style, no AI-generated media. Custom backgrounds and other parameters can be set in the template.
t.template.type.image_hint: AI automatically generates matching illustrations based on the script; size is determined by the template.
t.template.type.video_hint: AI automatically generates matching video segments based on the script; size is determined by the template.
t.template.preview_default_text: Pixelle.AI is transforming content creation with AI, letting everyone easily produce professional-grade videos.
t.template.no_templates_with_preview: ⚠️ No templates available for this type
t.image.not_required: The current template does not require illustration generation
t.image.not_required_hint: Your selected template is text-only, no image generation is needed. This will: ⚡ speed up generation 💰 lower cost
t.video.title: 🎬 Video Settings
t.video.frames_fixed_mode_hint: 💡 Fixed mode: number of scenes is determined by the actual paragraph count
t.bgm.volume_help: Adjust BGM volume (0.0 = mute, 1.0 = original volume)
t.bgm.what: Add background music to the video for atmosphere and professionalism
t.bgm.how: Place audio files (MP3/WAV/FLAC, etc.) into the bgm/ folder for auto-detection

t.batch.mode_help: Generate multiple videos in batch, one topic per line
t.batch.rule_3: All videos use the same configuration (TTS, template, workflow, etc.)

Why develop a reading habit
How to manage time efficiently

Building good relationships

t.action_transfer.assets.video_what: Upload the reference video for action transfer
t.action_transfer.assets.video_how: Supports MP4/MKV/MOV video formats. The reference video only supports single-person action transfer; obvious action performance is recommended
t.action_transfer.assets.video_upload_help: Only a single video upload is supported, and the video must contain only one person. Video length must be ≤ 30 seconds; if longer than 30 seconds, only the first 30 seconds will be used as reference
t.action_transfer.assets.video_empty_hint: 💡 Please upload a reference video for action transfer
t.action_transfer.assets.image_what: Upload the image to which actions will be transferred
t.action_transfer.assets.image_how: Supports JPG/PNG/WebP image formats; assets should be clear and relevant
t.action_transfer.assets.image_upload_help: Only single image upload is supported; works best with single-person images
t.action_transfer.assets.image_empty_hint: 💡 Please upload an image for action transfer
t.action_transfer.input.topic_placeholder: Enter creation prompt (e.g., imitate the reference video dancing, keep position, match steps in sync, etc.)
t.action_transfer.workflow_select: Action-Transfer Workflow Selection (includes local and RunningHub)
t.action_transfer.assets.image_warning: Upload an image for action transfer
t.action_transfer.assets.video_warning: Upload a reference video for action transfer
t.selfhost.warning.title: You selected a SelfHost (local) workflow. Please confirm the following first:
t.selfhost.warning.message:
1. First load and run `{workflow_path}` standalone in your ComfyUI environment ({comfyui_url})
2. Make sure the workflow runs successfully without missing nodes or models
3. After the workflow is verified, return to this project to use it
t.selfhost.warning.hint: ⚠️ If you have not verified this workflow in ComfyUI, subsequent runs **will fail** (typically with a 400 error)! Do not skip this step.
